ddls:
CREATE TABLE "jobs" (
  "job_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job posting */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the job */,
  "type" INTEGER /* Type of job (e.g., Full Time, Part Time) */,
  "location" INTEGER /* Location of the job */,
  "remote" INTEGER /* Remote work arrangement (e.g., Hybrid, Onsite) */,
  "pay_rate" TEXT /* Pay rate for the job */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the job */,
  "recruiter_id" INTEGER /* ID of the recruiter managing the job posting */,
  "required_skills" TEXT /* Required skills for the job */,
  "preferred_skills" TEXT /* Preferred skills for the job */,
  "job_status" TEXT /* Status of the job posting (e.g., Open, Closed, Filled) */,
  "experience_level" TEXT /* Experience level required for the job */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("job_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_jobs_recruiter_id FOREIGN KEY ("recruiter_id") REFERENCES recruiters ("recruiter_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_jobs_location FOREIGN KEY ("location") REFERENCES locations ("location_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_jobs_remote FOREIGN KEY ("remote") REFERENCES remote_arrangements ("arrangement_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_jobs_type FOREIGN KEY ("type") REFERENCES job_types ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the job type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the job type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "location_name" TEXT /* Name of the location */,
  "city" TEXT /* City of the location */,
  "state" TEXT /* State of the location */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country of the location */,
  "zip_code" TEXT /* Zip code of the location */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "remote_arrangements" (
  "arrangement_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each remote arrangement */,
  "arrangement_name" TEXT /* Name of the remote arrangement */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the remote arrangement */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("arrangement_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "applicants" (
  "applicant_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each applicant */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the applicant */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the applicant */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the applicant */,
  "resume" TEXT /* Applicant's resume */,
  "cover_letter" TEXT /* Applicant's cover letter */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the applicant */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the applicant */,
  "city" TEXT /* City of the applicant */,
  "state" TEXT /* State of the applicant */,
  "zip_code" TEXT /* Zip code of the applicant */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country of the applicant */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("applicant_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "applications" (
  "application_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each application */,
  "job_id" INTEGER /* ID of the job posting applied for */,
  "applicant_id" INTEGER /* ID of the applicant */,
  "submission_date" TEXT /* Date the application was submitted */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the application (e.g., Pending, Rejected, Accepted) */,
  "interview_status" TEXT /* Status of the interview process */,
  "interview_date" TEXT /* Date of the interview */,
  "interviewer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the recruiter who conducted the interview */,
  "interview_notes" TEXT /* Notes from the interview */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("application_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_applications_job_id FOREIGN KEY ("job_id") REFERENCES jobs ("job_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_applications_interviewer_id FOREIGN KEY ("interviewer_id") REFERENCES recruiters ("recruiter_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_applications_applicant_id FOREIGN KEY ("applicant_id") REFERENCES applicants ("applicant_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "recruiters" (
  "recruiter_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each recruiter */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the recruiter */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the recruiter */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the recruiter */,
  "team_id" INTEGER /* ID of the recruitment team the recruiter belongs to */,
  "hiring_manager_id" INTEGER /* ID of the hiring manager the recruiter works with */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("recruiter_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_posting_history" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each history entry */,
  "job_id" INTEGER /* ID of the job posting */,
  "posting_date" TEXT /* Date the job was posted */,
  "closing_date" TEXT /* Date the job posting closed */,
  "recruiter_id" INTEGER /* ID of the recruiter who posted the job */,
  "number_of_applicants" INTEGER /* Number of applicants for the job posting */,
  "average_application_rating" REAL /* Average rating of applications for the job posting */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_posting_history_job_id FOREIGN KEY ("job_id") REFERENCES jobs ("job_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_posting_history_recruiter_id FOREIGN KEY ("recruiter_id") REFERENCES recruiters ("recruiter_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "recipient_id" INTEGER /* ID of the recipient (recruiter or applicant) */,
  "notification_type" TEXT /* Type of notification (e.g., new application, job posting update) */,
  "notification_date" TEXT /* Date the notification was sent */,
  "message" TEXT /* Content of the notification */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_recipient_id FOREIGN KEY ("recipient_id") REFERENCES applicants ("applicant_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_recipient_id FOREIGN KEY ("recipient_id") REFERENCES recruiters ("recruiter_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of report (e.g., job posting trends, applicant demographics) */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date the report was generated */,
  "data" TEXT /* Report data */,
  "generated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who generated the report */,
  "recipient_id" INTEGER /* ID of the recipient of the report (if applicable) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_recipient_id FOREIGN KEY ("recipient_id") REFERENCES applicants ("applicant_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_recipient_id FOREIGN KEY ("recipient_id") REFERENCES recruiters ("recruiter_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_generated_by FOREIGN KEY ("generated_by") REFERENCES recruiters ("recruiter_id")
)
